warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.sourceTitle: Standard for Food Additive Use - GB2760-2015
rules.notes: This standard specifies the varieties, scope of use, and maximum dosages of food additives. The full standard document must be consulted for detailed limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standard for Food Additive Use - GB2760-2015

rules.parameter: Compliance with GB Food Safety Standards
rules.sourceTitle: National Health Commission of the People's Republic of China
rules.notes: The National Health Commission is responsible for developing and overseeing food safety standards (GB standards) in China. Specific standards must be consulted for detailed requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: 食品安全标准与监测评估司

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the online database and its Preamble for specific provisions.
rules.evidenceQuote: The 'Codex General Standard for Food Additives' (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods

rules.limit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
rules.notes: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration legally permitted. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses these levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.limit: Follow Code of Practice for Prevention and Reduction
rules.notes: A specific Code of Practice (CXC 78-2017) exists for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices.
rules.evidenceQuote: CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices

rules.notes: Food additives approved for use in the EU and their conditions of use must comply with the Union list of food additives, which is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Food safety criteria and process hygiene criteria for foods are laid down in Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة
rules.notes: This regulation specifies the mandatory information that must be present on the labels of pre-packaged food products.
rules.evidenceQuote: بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.parameter: Mandatory shelf life periods for food products
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية
rules.notes: This regulation specifies the mandatory shelf life periods for various food products, which must be adhered to and declared on labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية

rules.foodCategory: Prepackaged food stuffs (includes seasoning, snack food, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 - Labeling of prepackaged food stuffs
rules.notes: Specific labelling requirements for prepackaged food products, including mandatory information, must be verified by consulting the full text of GSO 9:2022.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.foodCategory: Halal Food (includes seasoning, snack food, processed foods intended to be Halal)
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 - HALAL FOOD - Part 1 : General Requirements
rules.notes: General requirements for Halal food, including sourcing, processing, and labelling, must be verified by consulting the full text of GSO 2055-1:2015.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال

rules.foodCategory: Food products (includes seasoning, snack food, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 - Expiration dates for food products -Part 1 : Mandatory expiration dates
rules.notes: Mandatory expiration date requirements for food products must be verified by consulting the full text of GSO 150-1:2013.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية

rules.foodCategory: Food products (includes seasoning, snack food, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-2:2013 - Expiration dates for food products - Part 2 : Voluntary expiration dates
rules.notes: Guidelines for voluntary expiration dates for food products must be verified by consulting the full text of GSO 150-2:2013.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-2:2013 مواصفة قياسية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية

rules.foodCategory: Foodstuffs intended for human consumption and food ingredients (includes seasoning, snack food, processed foods)
rules.notes: Specific microbiological limits for various foodstuffs and food ingredients must be verified by consulting the full text of GSO 1016:2015.
rules.evidenceQuote: This GSO technical regulation is concerned with microbiological limits for some foodstuffs intended for human consumption and for some food ingredients used in food industry.

rules.limit: Verification required as per FSSAI regulations.
rules.notes: Specific additive limits for various food categories, including seasoning, snack food, and ready-to-eat snacks, are detailed in the FSSAI Compendium of Food Additives Regulations. Verification of the specific additive and food category is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Food_Additives_Regulations

rules.limit: Verification required as per FSSAI regulations.
rules.notes: General labelling and display requirements for food products, including seasoning, snack food, and ready-to-eat snacks, are detailed in the FSSAI Compendium of Labelling & Display Regulations. Verification of specific labelling elements is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Labelling_Display

rules.sourceTitle: Surat Edaran Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 tentang Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)
rules.notes: This circular letter outlines the provisions for the production, import, registration, and circulation of Dinitrogen Monoxide (N2O) as a food additive. Specific limits would be detailed within the full regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)

rules.notes: BPOM has 281 regulations concerning processed foods, which include provisions for food additive limits. Specific limits must be verified within these regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.sourceTitle: Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan (Pangan Olahan)
rules.notes: BPOM has 281 regulations concerning processed foods, which likely include provisions for allergen declaration. Specific requirements must be verified within these regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.sourceTitle: Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan (Pangan Olahan)
rules.notes: BPOM has 281 regulations concerning processed foods, which include provisions for contaminant limits. Specific limits must be verified within these regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.sourceTitle: PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan
rules.notes: This regulation defines food categories, which are fundamental for applying specific rules and for correct product labelling.
rules.evidenceQuote: PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan

rules.sourceTitle: Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan (Pangan Olahan)
rules.notes: BPOM has 281 regulations concerning processed foods, which include provisions for microbiological limits. Specific limits must be verified within these regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.sourceTitle: Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan (Pangan Olahan)
rules.notes: BPOM has 281 regulations concerning processed foods, which may include provisions for salt/sodium limits. Specific limits must be verified within these regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.parameter: Imported food and additives requiring specific process controls or certification
rules.limit: Must be produced or processed in MHLW-specified countries/regions/facilities, or have certificates from the exporting country. Verification required.
rules.notes: This applies to food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order.
rules.evidenceQuote: The food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order as requiring measures to control particularly important processes... must not be imported... unless they have been produced or processed in the countries or regions, or facilities specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as actually taking those measures.

rules.limit: Only additives specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as unlikely to cause harm to human health are permitted for sale, production, import, processing, use, storage, or display. Verification required.
rules.notes: Excludes natural flavoring agents and substances generally served for human consumption and used as additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and things that have generally been served for human consumption, and are used as additives) and preparations and food containing additives must not be sold... except for cases that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.parameter: Production, processing, use, cooking, or preservation methods for food or additives; ingredients of food or additives
rules.limit: Must conform to criteria and standards established by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare. Verification required.
rules.notes: These standards and criteria can cover various aspects including additive limits, microbiological safety, and contaminant levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish the criteria for the methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food or additives... or may establish standards for the ingredients of food or additives... When the criteria or standards have been established... food or additives must not be produced... using methods that do not conform to such criteria; food or additives that do not conform to such criteria must not be sold or imported; and food or additives that do not conform to such standards must not be produced, imported... or sold.

rules.limit: Must be clean, sanitary, and not contain or bear toxic or harmful substances, or have a harmful effect on food/additives. Must conform to MHLW-established standards/criteria. Verification required.
rules.notes: This applies to apparatus, containers and packaging used in business or sold.
rules.evidenceQuote: Apparatus, and containers and packaging used in conducting business must be clean and sanitary.

rules.parameter: General hygiene for food and additives
rules.limit: Must be collected, produced, processed, used, cooked, stored, transported, displayed and delivered in a clean and sanitary manner.
rules.notes: This is a fundamental requirement to prevent contamination.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food or additives which are meant to be sold... must be collected, produced, processed, used, cooked, stored, transported, displayed and delivered in a clean and sanitary manner.

rules.parameter: Imported food and additives requiring specific process controls or certification
rules.limit: Must be produced or processed in MHLW-specified countries/regions/facilities, or have certificates from the exporting country. Verification required.
rules.notes: This applies to food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order.
rules.evidenceQuote: The food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order as requiring measures to control particularly important processes... must not be imported... unless they have been produced or processed in the countries or regions, or facilities specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as actually taking those measures.

rules.parameter: Production, processing, use, cooking, or preservation methods for food or additives; ingredients of food or additives
rules.limit: Must conform to criteria and standards established by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare. Verification required.
rules.notes: These standards and criteria can cover various aspects including additive limits, microbiological safety, and contaminant levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish the criteria for the methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food or additives... or may establish standards for the ingredients of food or additives... When the criteria or standards have been established... food or additives must not be produced... using methods that do not conform to such criteria; food or additives that do not conform to such criteria must not be sold or imported; and food or additives that do not conform to such standards must not be produced, imported... or sold.

rules.parameter: Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, and animal pharmaceuticals
rules.limit: Must not remain in a quantity exceeding the quantity specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as unlikely to cause harm to human health, or established standards. Verification required.
rules.notes: This applies unless specific standards for residual limits are established under Article 13, paragraph (1).
rules.evidenceQuote: Food in which substances that are the ingredients of agricultural chemicals... feed... and pharmaceutical products... remain in a quantity exceeding the quantity that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health... must not be produced, imported... or sold

rules.limit: Not allowed to contain or bear toxic or harmful substances, or be suspected to, unless specified by MHLW as harmless.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which contain or bear toxic or harmful substances or are suspected to contain or bear such substances; provided, however, that this does not apply when the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies that the substances are unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.parameter: Notification of harm from designated ingredients
rules.limit: Businesses handling food containing MHLW-designated ingredients must notify the relevant prefectural governor, etc., without delay if they obtain information that the food causes or may cause harm to human health.
rules.notes: This is a mandatory notification requirement for potential safety hazards.
rules.evidenceQuote: A business that handles food containing any ingredient or material which requires special attention... upon obtaining information that any food containing designated ingredient, etc. handled by the business causes or may cause harm to human health, must notify the relevant prefectural governor, etc. of that information without delay

rules.parameter: General hygiene for food and additives
rules.limit: Must be collected, produced, processed, used, cooked, stored, transported, displayed and delivered in a clean and sanitary manner.
rules.notes: This is a fundamental requirement to prevent microbiological contamination.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food or additives which are meant to be sold... must be collected, produced, processed, used, cooked, stored, transported, displayed and delivered in a clean and sanitary manner.

rules.parameter: Imported food and additives requiring specific process controls or certification
rules.limit: Must be produced or processed in MHLW-specified countries/regions/facilities, or have certificates from the exporting country. Verification required.
rules.notes: This applies to food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order.
rules.evidenceQuote: The food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order as requiring measures to control particularly important processes... must not be imported... unless they have been produced or processed in the countries or regions, or facilities specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as actually taking those measures.

rules.limit: Not allowed to be contaminated with pathogens or suspected to be so, if likely to cause harm to human health.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are contaminated with pathogens or are suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health

rules.parameter: Production, processing, use, cooking, or preservation methods for food or additives; ingredients of food or additives
rules.limit: Must conform to criteria and standards established by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare. Verification required.
rules.notes: These standards and criteria can cover various aspects including additive limits, microbiological safety, and contaminant levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish the criteria for the methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food or additives... or may establish standards for the ingredients of food or additives... When the criteria or standards have been established... food or additives must not be produced... using methods that do not conform to such criteria; food or additives that do not conform to such criteria must not be sold or imported; and food or additives that do not conform to such standards must not be produced, imported... or sold.

rules.limit: Not allowed to be rotten or deteriorated, unless unlikely to cause harm and recognized as edible.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are rotten or have deteriorated, or are immature; provided, however, that this does not apply to those which are unlikely to cause harm to human health in general and are recognized as edible

rules.parameter: Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
rules.notes: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are used in food, the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives must be used to determine permitted levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food (e.g. sorbic acid or benzoic acid), use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels that should be used.

rules.notes: Food additives must undergo safety assessment by SFA before being used in food sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.notes: Food products containing an additive that is not permitted must not be imported or sold.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Contaminants in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Pesticide residues must comply with Singapore's regulatory limits or Codex MRLs if no local limits are specified. Verification is required using the relevant SFA documents or Codex MRLs.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pesticide residues Singapore's regulatory limits Codex MRLs (if there are no regulatory limits specified in the Food Regulations)

rules.parameter: Various contaminants (Microbiological, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins, Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, Other incidental constituents)
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Verification of specific limits for various contaminants is required by referring to the relevant SFA documents.
rules.evidenceQuote: For more details, please refer to the relevant documents below: Microbiological standards Ready-to-eat food Non-ready-to-eat food (till 3 Mar 2024) Non-ready-to-eat food (from 4 Mar 2024) Heavy metals Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins Veterinary drug residues Pesticide residues Other incidental constituents

rules.foodCategory: Meat and any food derived from meat
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat containing residues of specific oestrogen compounds is prohibited.
rules.evidenceQuote: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of the following compounds: diethylstibestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hexene) hexoestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-n-hexane) dienoestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-2, 4-hexadiene)

rules.parameter: Veterinary drug residues (other than prohibited oestrogens)
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Maximum Residue Limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues have been established and must be complied with. Verification is required by downloading the MRLs from the SFA website.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.notes: For prepacked foods, all ingredients and additives used must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Verification of microbiological standards for ready-to-eat food is required by referring to the relevant SFA documents.
rules.evidenceQuote: Microbiological standards Ready-to-eat food

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods
rules.notes: Specific limits for pesticide residues in foods must be verified in the referenced standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products
rules.notes: Specific limits for pesticide residues in animal products must be verified in the referenced standard. Relevant if animal products are used as ingredients.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products

rules.parameter: Cephalopods and cephalopod products, Spiral shells and Spiral shell products, Seeds and seed products (excluding highly refined/purified sunflower seed oil), Kiwis and kiwi products
rules.limit: Recommended if present as ingredient or food additive
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Food allergen labeling is recommended for these items when present as an ingredient or food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive: (1) Cephalopods and cephalopod products... (2) Spiral shells and Spiral shell products thereof... (3) Seeds and seed products... (4) Kiwis and kiwi products thereof...

rules.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
rules.limit: Required if present as ingredient or food additive
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Mandatory allergen labeling for these items when present as an ingredient or food additive in prepackaged foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: For prepackaged foods, food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive... Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish

rules.sourceTitle: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products
rules.notes: Specific requirements for nutrition claims on prepackaged food products must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: A precautionary allergen labeling statement is suggested if there is a risk of cross-contamination during production.
rules.evidenceQuote: If there is a risk of a food product being affected by allergen cross-contamination... the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.

rules.limit: 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Mandatory allergen labeling for sulphite when the residue sulphur dioxide concentration is 10 mg/kg or more in prepackaged foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food

rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: Specific use restrictions and labeling requirements for γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) as a food ingredient must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient

rules.parameter: Permitted food additives and their limits
rules.notes: Specific lists and limits for food additives must be verified against official notifications and regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร

rules.parameter: Contaminants, toxins, and veterinary drug residues
rules.notes: Specific limits for contaminants, toxins, and veterinary drug residues must be verified against official food standards.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารปนเปื้อน

rules.parameter: General food labeling and nutrition labeling
rules.notes: Requirements for food labeling and nutrition labeling must be verified against official regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: การแสดงฉลากอาหารและฉลากโภชนาการ

rules.notes: Specific limits for pathogenic microorganisms must be verified against official food standards.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารด้านจุลินทรีย์ที่ทำให้เกิดโรค

rules.notes: Food and color additives are listed in FDA regulations and inventories, requiring verification for specific uses and limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Approved Food and Color Additives and Petitions Under Review

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Delisted color additives are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30) and the Substances Added to Food inventory.
rules.evidenceQuote: Delisted color additives in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30) (labeled as "DELISTED").

rules.parameter: Food additives, color additives, GRAS substances, prior-sanctioned substances
rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: The Substances Added to Food inventory includes various regulated ingredients, requiring verification against specific CFR parts.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively)... Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Prohibited substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) and the Substances Added to Food inventory.
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food (labeled as "PROHIBITED" or "PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS").

rules.foodCategory: seasonings, snack foods, processed foods (most packaged food products)
rules.notes: The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame. All FDA requirements apply to sesame as of January 1, 2023.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.notes: This is a guidance level for 3-MCPD.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm CPG 500.500

rules.notes: This is an action level for total aflatoxins in general foods. Specific food categories like Brazil nuts, peanuts, pistachio nuts also have this action level.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb CPG 555.400

rules.parameter: Poisonous or deleterious substances (e.g., toxic elements, mycotoxins, persistent pesticides, radionuclides)
rules.notes: The tool provides a consolidated list of contaminant levels including tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA has published a variety of contaminant levels for poisonous or deleterious substances in human food in various guidance documents and regulations for industry. For ease of reference, the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list of these levels.

rules.notes: FDA has a final rule defining 'gluten-free' for food labeling (August 2, 2013), including compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods (August 12, 2020).
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling... On August 12, 2020, the FDA issued a final rule to establish compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods...

rules.notes: Advisory statements are not required by law and should only be used to address unavoidable cross-contact after good manufacturing practices have been incorporated and precautions taken. They must be truthful and not misleading.
rules.evidenceQuote: Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility and have taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact...

rules.notes: The CGMP & PC rule (21 CFR part 117) establishes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens. Facilities must have written procedures.
rules.evidenceQuote: The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

rules.notes: Cochineal extract and carmine must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine... have been identified as allergenic substances that must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.foodCategory: seasonings, snack foods, processed foods (most packaged food products)
rules.notes: The food source of a major food allergen must appear in parentheses following the ingredient name OR immediately after or next to the ingredient list in a 'contains' statement. Specific types of tree nuts, fish, and Crustacean shellfish must be declared.
rules.evidenceQuote: The allergen's food source must be declared at least once on the food label in one of two ways. The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient... — OR — Immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement.

rules.notes: All products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 must identify it on their labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels so consumers who are sensitive to the dye can avoid it.

rules.notes: Sulfiting agents must be declared on food labels when present in food at a concentration of 10 ppm or more total sulfites.
rules.evidenceQuote: they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.
